name: Peach-Apricot Pie
recipeIngredient: 6 cups Sliced fresh peaches (8 to 10 medium)
recipeInstructions:
1. Make this southern favorite at summer's peak with juicy, ripe peaches.
2. Preheat oven to 425 degrees.
3. Prepare pastry dough according to package instructions.
4. In a large bowl, mix together the brown sugar, flour, and cinnamon.
5. Stir in the sliced peaches, apricot jam or preserves, and lemon juice until combined.
6. Turn the mixture into the prepared pie crust-lined pie plate.
7. Dot the top with small pieces of margarine.
8. Cover the filling with the top crust, making sure to cut slits in it for steam to escape.
9. Fold over the edge of the crust and flute as desired.
10. Place a 2- to 3-inch strip of aluminum foil around the edge of the pie plate to prevent excessive browning.
11. Bake in the preheated oven for about 45 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the juice begins to bubble through the slits in the crust.
12. Remove the foil during the last 15 minutes of baking.
13. Serve with ice cream and Raspberry-Currant Sauce if desired. Makes 8 servings; approximately 400 calories each for a 9-inch pie.
